stress_context: moderate and severe drought followed by rehydration
mechanism_note: Recovery after drought activates cell-state-specific immune programs and suppresses bacterial growth more strongly after moderate than severe stress
notes: Very high-value marker because it shows recovery is a biologically distinct state rather than simple reset.

stress_context: drought stress followed by 15-day rewatering window
recovery_signature: Drought Recovery Index (DRI) across SPAD/stem diameter/height
mechanism_note: Recovery differs strongly across genotypes; relative chlorophyll content and stem diameter recover more robustly than plant height or internode length
notes: Good panel anchor because it converts recovery into a comparative scoring system.

mechanism_note: ABA-related PYR/PYL-SnRK2 and multiple TF families remain implicated during re-watering; some DEGs persist beyond drought itself
notes: Useful for identifying recovery-linked transcription factors rather than only stress-time markers.

mechanism_note: Hydraulic conductance can fully recover, but the time required depends strongly on drought duration
notes: Core resilience marker because water transport recovery is central and quantifiable.

mechanism_note: Leaf functional traits do not always fully recover after rewatering; restoration strength is duration-dependent
notes: Important negative marker showing resilience is limited by stress duration.

recovery_signature: rehydration capacity via relative water content (RWC)
mechanism_note: After rewatering, leaves can rehydrate toward irrigated levels, but cultivar capacity differs and some lines remain drought-like
notes: Useful for genotype-level recovery ranking under practical conditions.

mechanism_note: Leaf transpiration and stomatal conductance after rewatering approach fully irrigated levels in recovery-capable tomatoes
notes: Good functional counterpart to RWC-based rehydration.
